title: auth — feature keys
description: Auth-facing feature keys surfaced in the /auth/me bootstrap payload. The SPA reads these to decide which UI flows to render (cross-app switcher, session-management screen, MFA-enrol wizard entry, impersonation banner).

features.description: Base auth capability. Always on. Presence indicates auth-service is reachable.
Self-service listing + revocation of one's own sessions (GET/DELETE /auth/sessions). Always on.
Rotation-with-reuse-detection on every refresh. Composite of the refresh_token_rotation entitlement. Silent gate.
Grant-based cross-Application session issuance. When on, the SPA renders an Application switcher; when off, the switcher is hidden (Free tier).
PAT lifetime extended to auth.session.extended_ttl_days. Enterprise-only.
Email + in-app alerts on novel-device logins. Team+ tier.
Email alerts on novel-GeoIP-location logins. Composite gate: new_device_notifications entitlement AND the User has opted in.
Runtime flag — present in /auth/me ONLY when a platform-staff impersonation session is currently active on the caller's User. When present, the SPA renders a persistent banner.
features.notes: This key is dynamic — evaluated at each /auth/me call. Absent when no active impersonation session; present + `active=true` when one exists.

features.description: Composite of the identity.mfa feature key (from identity substrate) + auth's session-lifecycle wiring. Signals the SPA that MFA-enrol screens are reachable.
